title: Bio-Modelling Project — Contents Manifest
description: Index of all models, modules, scripts, exports, and figures in the anti-NMDAR encephalitis Petri-Net study with downstream neurodegeneration pathway.

sections.title: Pipeline drivers (entry points)
sections.description: Top-level scripts that orchestrate model building, simulation, figure generation, and export.

items.title: Orchestrator
items.subtitle: Runs both NMDAR and neurodegeneration pipelines sequentially. Accepts a shared CFG dict for global settings (seeds, FPS, t_max, PDF mode).

items.title: NMDAR pipeline driver
items.subtitle: End-to-end driver for the anti-NMDAR encephalitis Petri-Net study. Builds baseline + perturbation models, runs Gillespie SSA, produces static PNG/PDF, animated GIFs, Snoopy XML (.spn / .cpn), and Charlie APNN/ANDL exports.

items.title: Neurodegeneration pipeline driver
items.subtitle: End-to-end driver for the downstream Ca²⁺ → CASP3 / neurodegeneration model. Builds baseline + perturbation models, runs Gillespie SSA, produces static figures, animated GIFs, and Charlie APNN/ANDL exports.

sections.title: Core models (Petri-Net definitions)
sections.description: Model builder functions that construct PetriNet objects with places, transitions, arcs, rate constants, and layout coordinates.

items.title: NMDAR signalling model builders
items.subtitle: build_baseline() — Healthy NMDAR signalling at a glutamatergic synapse. build_perturbation() — Anti-NMDAR encephalitis with antibody binding, bivalent crosslinking, EphB2 disruption, clathrin endocytosis, and lysosomal degradation. Both share identical housekeeping species; perturbation adds disease-specific machinery on column X[8].

items.title: Downstream neurodegeneration model builders
items.subtitle: build_downstream_baseline() — Healthy neuron with resting Ca²⁺ levels driving low basal activity through mitochondrial ROS, ER stress/CHOP, CASP9 → CASP3 cascade. build_downstream_perturbation() — Anti-NMDAR encephalitis state with reduced Ca²⁺ but elevated ROS and ER stress through maladaptive compensation, leading to disproportionate CASP3 accumulation and neurodegeneration. Uses shared _build() internal function parameterised by rate constants and initial Ca²⁺ tokens.

sections.title: Core framework (Petri-Net engine)
sections.description: Lightweight, self-contained Petri-Net implementation powering all models.

items.title: Petri-Net core library
items.subtitle: Place, Arc, Transition dataclasses and PetriNet class with: discrete integer tokens with stoichiometric arcs, mass-action propensities for Gillespie SSA, compartment metadata for layered matplotlib visualisation, capacity constraints, per-element rate annotation and citation tags. COMPARTMENT_COLORS palette maps compartment names to matplotlib colours.

items.title: Gillespie stochastic simulation engine
items.subtitle: gillespie() — Standard direct-method SSA returning (time, transition, marking) trace. sample_trace() — Left-continuous step interpolation onto a time grid for plotting. cumulative_ca_influx() — Cumulative firing count of a specified transition (used for Ca²⁺ influx and neurodegeneration accumulation curves).

sections.title: Visualisation and comparison figures
sections.description: matplotlib-based renderers for static diagrams, animated GIFs, and side-by-side comparisons.

items.title: NMDAR Petri-Net visualisation and animation
items.subtitle: draw_static() — Single annotated Petri net PNG/PDF with compartment bands, rate labels, citations, and legend. animate() — Gillespie token-flow animation as GIF with firing-highlight transitions and HUD. animate_combined() — Dual-panel GIF: Petri net (left) + synchronised instantaneous/cumulative Ca²⁺ curves (right). draw_nmdar_pn_comparison() / animate_nmdar_pn_comparison() — Side-by-side baseline + perturbation Petri nets with cumulative Ca²⁺ curve underneath (static or animated). Includes subnet-block rendering for hierarchical coarse-node links.

items.title: Ca²⁺ influx comparison figures
items.subtitle: static_comparison() — Two-panel PNG: instantaneous cytosolic Ca²⁺ (top) and cumulative T_Ca_in firings (bottom) with ratio annotation. animated_comparison() — Animated GIF of the same curves growing in real time with HUD showing per-frame cumulative values and perturbation/baseline ratio.

items.title: Neurodegeneration comparison figures
items.subtitle: render_petri_nets() — Static side-by-side downstream Petri net diagram. animate_downstream_petri_nets() — Animated side-by-side Petri nets + synchronised cumulative neurodegeneration curve. render_comparison() — Ensemble bar chart + absolute difference (perturbation − baseline) across 100 SSA runs for CHOP, ROS, CASP3, Apoptosis tokens/firings, and T_Neurodegeneration. render_trajectory_comparison() — Five-panel static trajectory (CASP3, CHOP, ROS, Apoptosis, cumulative ND). render_trajectory_gif() — Animated version of the trajectory curves.

sections.title: External tool exporters
sections.description: Modules that convert the Python Petri-Net models into formats readable by third-party analysis tools.

items.title: Snoopy XML exporter
items.subtitle: Writes Snoopy native XML files (.spn for Stochastic Petri Nets, .cpn for Continuous Petri Nets) with full metadata: place/transition attributes, MassAction(k) rate functions, scaled layout positions, edge multiplicities, General/SimulationProperties/Plot metadata sections. Uses CDATA-safe XML serialisation. Entry points: export_spn(), export_cpn(), export_pair(). Can be run directly as python src/snoopy_export.py.

items.title: Charlie APNN and ANDL exporter
items.subtitle: export_apnn() — Charlie-readable APNN format matching the reference cs1.apnn: \name labels, \capacity, \init markings, arcs with \weight{w} \type{ordinary}. export_andl() — ANDL format (Charlie's native textual format) carrying arc weights natively via [Place +/- w] terms, correct for weight>1 arcs. export_legend() — TSV mapping of sanitised APNN/ANDL identifiers to human-readable model names. Includes runnable __main__ block.
